title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 38 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Brazil's official start to the new year, celebrated nationwide with fireworks, beach parties, and New Year's Eve traditions carried into January 1st.

festivals.name: Festa de Iemanjá
festivals.description: A spiritual celebration honoring Iemanjá, the Afro-Brazilian goddess of the sea, with processions, offerings, and beach ceremonies along the northeastern coast.

festivals.name: Carnival of Rio de Janeiro
festivals.description: The world's most famous carnival featuring elaborate samba school parades with millions of spectators, dazzling floats, and non-stop celebrations across Rio.

festivals.name: Carnival of Salvador
festivals.description: Brazil's oldest and wildest carnival with massive street parades, trio elétricos (mobile stages), and Afro-Brazilian cultural performances in Salvador's historic streets.

festivals.name: Carnival of Recife and Olinda
festivals.description: A historic carnival featuring traditional frevo dances, giant puppets called bonecões, and street festivities across the colonial towns of Recife and Olinda.

festivals.name: Carnival of Manaus
festivals.description: The Amazon's vibrant carnival celebration blending indigenous and African rhythms with samba schools and boat parades along the Rio Negro.

festivals.name: Festa de São Jorge
festivals.description: A religious celebration honoring Saint George with processions, feasts, and traditional foods in churches and communities throughout southeastern Brazil.

festivals.name: Tiradentes' Day
festivals.description: A national holiday commemorating the execution of revolutionary Tiradentes with historical reenactments, parades, and civic ceremonies across Brazil.

festivals.name: Festival de Parintins
festivals.description: A spectacular celebration of Amazonian culture featuring two rival indigenous-inspired dance companies, Caprichoso and Garantido, with elaborate costumes and choreography.

festivals.name: Festa Junina - June Festivals
festivals.description: Traditional celebrations across Brazil honoring Catholic saints with street parties, traditional foods like corn dishes, music, and dancing in regional styles.

festivals.name: Festa de Santo Antônio
festivals.description: A religious festival honoring Saint Anthony with processions and celebrations particularly vibrant in the Northeast, involving matchmaking traditions and blessings.

festivals.name: Festa de São João
festivals.description: The most beloved June festival celebrating Saint John with bonfires, traditional quadrilha dancing, and regional cuisine throughout northeastern Brazil.

festivals.name: Festa de São Pedro
festivals.description: A saint's day celebration honoring the patron saint of fishermen with boat blessings, beach festivals, and traditional festivities in coastal communities.

festivals.name: Corpus Christi
festivals.description: A religious holiday celebrated with street processions, carpet decorations made of flowers and colored sawdust, and masses in Catholic communities.

festivals.name: Festa da Iemanjá em Maceió
festivals.description: A major Afro-Brazilian religious celebration honoring the sea goddess with offerings thrown into the ocean, processions, and spiritual ceremonies in Alagoas.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: Brazil's national independence day with parades, civic ceremonies, flag displays, and celebrations commemorating independence from Portugal in 1822.

festivals.name: Festa da Penha
festivals.description: A pilgrimage festival honoring Nossa Senhora da Penha with processions, masses, and gatherings at the Penha sanctuary in Recife.

festivals.name: Festa de Nossa Senhora da Conceição
festivals.description: A religious celebration honoring the Virgin Mary with processions, masses, and traditional festivities across northern Brazil.

festivals.name: Festa da Chiquita Bacana
festivals.description: A vibrant cultural festival celebrating Amazonian traditions, indigenous culture, and regional music with street performances and craft displays.

festivals.name: Festa Nacional de Folclore
festivals.description: A national folklore festival celebrating Brazilian traditional culture, crafts, music, and regional traditions from across the country.

festivals.name: Festa de São Benedito
festivals.description: A saint's day celebration with processions and traditional festivities honoring the Black Saint Benedict in southern Brazilian communities.

festivals.name: Aniversário de São Paulo
festivals.description: São Paulo's birthday celebration featuring parades, street festivals, cultural events, and concerts throughout Brazil's largest city.

festivals.name: Festival de Música de Manaus
festivals.description: An international music festival held in the historic Teatro Amazonas showcasing classical, jazz, and world music performances in the Amazon.

festivals.name: Festa do Açaí
festivals.description: A celebration of the Amazonian açaí fruit with food stalls, traditional recipes, and cultural performances showcasing regional cuisine.

festivals.name: Festival Gastronômico de Minas Gerais
festivals.description: A celebration of traditional Mineiro cuisine featuring cheese, cachaca, and regional dishes in festivals throughout the state.

festivals.name: Festa da Gastronomia
festivals.description: A southern Brazilian food festival celebrating regional cuisine including churrasco, wine, and traditional dishes from Rio Grande do Sul.

festivals.name: Festa da Banana
festivals.description: A regional festival celebrating banana production with food competitions, tastings, and family activities in Santa Catarina.

festivals.name: Festa de Piratas
festivals.description: A historical reenactment festival celebrating the pirate history of Santa Catarina with costumes, boat parades, and themed entertainment.

festivals.name: Festa do Divino Espírito Santo
festivals.description: A religious celebration with Portuguese roots honoring the Holy Spirit, featuring processions, blessings, and traditional feasts across southern Brazil.
festivals.location: Santa Catarina, Rio Grande do Sul

festivals.name: Octoberfest
festivals.description: Brazil's largest beer festival celebrating German heritage with traditional beer, German food, and music in the southern regions.

festivals.name: All Souls' Day
festivals.description: A national holiday when Brazilians visit cemeteries to honor deceased family members with flowers, prayers, and candlelight vigils.

festivals.name: Black Consciousness Day
festivals.description: A national holiday celebrating Afro-Brazilian culture, history, and achievements with cultural events, parades, and educational activities.

festivals.name: Christmas
festivals.description: Brazil's major Christian holiday celebrated with family gatherings, traditional meals, church services, and festive decorations across all regions.

festivals.name: Festa de Nossa Senhora Aparecida
festivals.description: Brazil's national religious holiday honoring the patron saint of Brazil with processions, masses, and pilgrimages to the basilica in Aparecida.

festivals.name: Festa de Santa Bárbara
festivals.description: A religious celebration honoring Saint Barbara with processions, blessings, and festivities in churches and communities throughout Brazil.

festivals.name: Labor Day
festivals.description: A national holiday celebrating workers' rights with parades, political rallies, and speeches in cities across Brazil.

festivals.name: Festa da Luz
festivals.description: A Christmas light festival in northeastern cities featuring illuminated displays, processions, and holiday celebrations.

festivals.name: Festa Literária Internacional de Paraty
festivals.description: A prestigious international literary festival bringing together Brazilian and world authors for readings, discussions, and cultural events in historic Paraty.
